title: Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 185) to advance responsible policies.
plain_summary: This resolution creates special rules for Congress to vote on a bill called H.R. 185. The resolution changes the original bill to protect two important programs that help people. It makes it much harder for Congress to cut Medicaid, which provides health care for low-income people, or SNAP, which helps families buy food. These protections would last until January 20, 2029.
last_action_text: Motion to Discharge Committee filed by Mr. Boyle (PA). Petition No: 119-3. (Discharge petition text with signatures.)

sections.title: Resolution Providing for Consideration
sections.text: This resolution sets up the rules for how the House of Representatives will consider bill H.R. 185 about responsible policies. Once this resolution passes, the House must immediately start discussing H.R. 185. Any procedural objections to considering the bill are ignored. A replacement version of the bill described in section 4 of this resolution will be treated as if it was already approved. The bill will be treated as if it has already been read out loud. Any procedural objections to parts of the changed bill are ignored. The House will move directly to a final vote on the bill with only two exceptions. First, there will be one hour of debate time split equally between the chair and top minority member of the Rules Committee or people they choose to speak for them. Second, there can be one motion to send the bill back to committee with instructions.

sections.title: Section 2 Rule Exceptions
sections.text: Two specific House rules will not apply when considering H.R. 185. These are clause 1(c) of rule XIX and clause 8 of rule XX.

sections.title: Section 3 Senate Notification
sections.text: The House Clerk must send a message to the Senate saying that the House passed H.R. 185. This message must be sent no later than one week after the House passes the bill.

sections.title: Section 4 Bill Replacement Text
sections.text: This section provides the replacement text that will become the new version of H.R. 185. The new text changes the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 by adding new rules. It says that neither the Senate nor the House can consider any budget reconciliation bill (a special type of budget bill) that reduces Medicaid enrollment or benefits for people in the Medicaid program. It also says they cannot consider any reconciliation bill that reduces eligibility or benefits for families in the supplemental nutrition assistance program, which helps people buy food. These protection rules will end on January 20, 2029. The replacement text also makes a small change to another part of the Congressional Budget Act to make sure these new rules work properly until January 20, 2029.
